author: Vaisman
message:
PR #45563: [XLA:GPU] Add RHS transpose fold test for dot

Imported from GitHub PR https://github.com/openxla/xla/pull/45563

Add test coverage for folding a transpose on the RHS operand of a dot.

Existing tests cover positive LHS transpose folding and a negative RHS case (TransposedNonContractingDimsDontFold). This adds the corresponding positive RHS case where the RHS contracting dimension is transposed.

📝 Summary of Changes
Added RhsTransposeFoldGemm to matmul_utils_test.cc.
The test covers CanFoldTransposeOperandIntoDot(*dot, 1) returning true for a valid RHS transpose fold case.

🎯 Justification
The function CanFoldTransposeOperandIntoDot handles both LHS (operand_idx=0)
and RHS (operand_idx=1) operands. Existing tests cover positive LHS folding
and a negative RHS case (TransposedNonContractingDimsDontFold), but the
positive RHS fold case was not tested. This adds the missing coverage.

🚀 Kind of Contribution
🧪 Tests

🧪 Unit Tests:
Added RhsTransposeFoldGemm test in matmul_utils_test.cc.
Tests that CanFoldTransposeOperandIntoDot returns true when the RHS
operand has a transpose on the contracting dimension.
Copybara import of the project:

--
4f7a68c8804473c35c7cb8094c57a9e52487261c by Vasili Svirski <vasili.svirski@gmail.com>:

[XLA:GPU] Add RHS transpose fold test for CanFoldTransposeOperandIntoDot

Add test coverage for folding a transpose on the RHS operand of a dot.

Existing tests cover positive LHS transpose folding and a negative RHS
case (TransposedNonContractingDimsDontFold). This adds the corresponding
positive RHS case where the contracting dimension is transposed.

Merging this change closes #45563

author: Levon Ter-Grigoryan
message:
[XLA:GPU] Add support for multi-GPU barrier at XLA runtime level.

This allows XLA to use a single barrier buffer for a unique devices clique, which is important since barrier buffer should be registered as a NCCL window, which is an expensive operation.

author: Haibo Huang
message:
Deprecate and remove the PJRT HostAllocator extension.

The HostAllocator extension is being removed as it is no longer used. The `PJRT_Extension_Type_HostAllocator` is marked as deprecated, and all associated implementation files and build rules are deleted.

author: Junwhan Ahn
message:
Use cords to store the payload of `xla.ifrt.Serialized`

Using `std::string` is wasteful in terms of memory usage since it forces copying the memory owned by the original cord. This is particularly true for users who keep a large cord containing many IFRT objects in memory and concurrently take its substring for IFRT SerDes.

The only downside is that `HloProgramSerDes` needs to flatten the cord to parse StableHLO. This can be addressed by introducing a variant of `mlir::stablehlo::deserializePortableArtifact()` that accepts `llvm::SourceMgr` in the upstream like `llvm::parseSourceFile` does.

PiperOrigin-RevId: 947365229

author: A. Unique TensorFlower
message:
[XLA] Add debug attributes to original value recovery table

This change adds debug attributes to the original value recovery table in HloModule, allowing for better control over debug logging at lowering.

- Defines DebugAttributes struct in HloModule.
- Adds support for serialization to proto and HLO text format.
- Includes unit tests for parsing and round-trip serialization.

author: Terry Heo
message:
Remove obsolete SHLO reference library and experimental StableHLO tests

- Delete the obsolete SHLO C++ reference library
  (`tensorflow/lite/experimental/shlo`), which is no longer active
  or used by LiteRT runtime or compiler pipelines.
- Remove deprecated StableHLO benchmark runner references and flags
  from experimental evaluation scripts.
- Clean up obsolete experimental SHLO targets from automated CI test
  exclusion lists and performance benchmarking configurations.

author: A. Unique TensorFlower
message:
[XLA] Add file cleanup for HLO isolation fusion debugger reference dumps.

Introduce a cleanup struct to manage temporary files created for fusion debugger reference outputs. Generate filenames for these reference dumps, placing them in either the test undeclared outputs directory or a temporary location, and register them for deletion upon cleanup.

author: Shyamli Agrawal
message:
Fix thread pool starvation deadlock in GPU autotuning.

When autotuning large HLO modules with many unique instruction groups, the thread pool can be exhausted by TuneConfigs scheduling task wrappers that block waiting for compilation futures.
